task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select the specified EEG recording file for playback
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI shows 'Playback' mode selected and the file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is loaded or being selected in the file chooser.

task_completion.description: Start the data stream and view the Time Series widget
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget is visible in the GUI, displaying scrolling EEG waveforms, with Channels 1 and 2 (Fp1/Fp2) clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Locate the first eye blink artifact in the recording
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI playback is paused or focused near the beginning of the recording (around 0.5 to 1.5 seconds), showing a large, rounded deflection (peak) in Channels 1 and 2.

task_completion.description: Save the identified timestamp to the output file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window shows the creation or editing of '/home/ga/blink_timestamp.txt' containing a numerical value around 0.624 (acceptable range is 0.0 to 2.124 due to tolerance).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenBCI GUI to find the artifact
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the OpenBCI GUI (e.g., clicking playback, viewing the time series) rather than just parsing the text file with a terminal script.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass visual inspection
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing a Python or bash script to programmatically find the 80uV threshold peak in the text file.

integrity.description: The output file was genuinely created with the result
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal command (like echo or nano) or a text editor is explicitly used to write the numerical timestamp to '/home/ga/blink_timestamp.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. The first eye blink artifact (large peak in Channel 1/Fp1) occurs at exactly 0.624 seconds. The task allows a tolerance of 1.5 seconds, meaning any saved timestamp between 0.0 and 2.124 seconds is technically considered correct, though the agent should ideally identify a value very close to 0.624. The agent must use the OpenBCI GUI to visually identify this, rather than writing a script to parse the data file.
